title: Weighing pros, cons of Celtics trading for Giannis Antetokounmpo
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Celtics consider trading for 31-year-old Giannis Antetokounmpo to boost title chances alongside Jayson Tatum.
Acquiring Antetokounmpo may require trading Jaylen Brown, affecting Celtics' roster depth and 3-point shooting.
Concerns exist over Antetokounmpo's age, injury history, and fit within the Celtics' current play style.

title: Harvard College faculty to vote on limiting number of As for students
author: Molly Farrar
bullets: Harvard faculty are voting on a proposal to limit As in undergraduate courses, addressing grade inflation concerns.
The proposal suggests capping As at 20% per class, with exceptions for four additional As, excluding A- grades.
Critics argue the proposal might encourage competition among students and be too simplistic for addressing grade inflation.

title: Truck driver dies days after crash on North Attleborough highway
author: Carson Lyle
bullets: Julio Flores, a truck driver from Pawtucket, died nearly a week after a crash on I-95 in North Attleborough.
The crash occurred late on May 5, involving a 2014 Volkswagen Jetta and Flores' 2005 Freightliner M2 truck.
State Police are investigating the incident; no updates have been provided on the Jetta's four occupants.

title: Sports Hub’s Mike Felger shares wild Red Sox trade rumor involving Mike Trout
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Radio host Mike Felger shared a rumor about the Red Sox's interest in trading for Angels star Mike Trout.
Trout, 34, is having a strong season with 11 home runs but carries risks due to injuries and a hefty contract.
Red Sox trade package might include Jarren Duran and Brayan Bello, but other teams could offer more for Trout.

title: Romeo Doubs on learning Josh McDaniels’ offense: ‘I can already see how he thinks’
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: Romeo Doubs is adapting to the New England Patriots' offensive system under Josh McDaniels after leaving the Packers.
Doubs recorded 724 yards and six touchdowns in his last season with Green Bay and is expected to be a key target for QB Drake Maye.
The Patriots are interested in trading for AJ Brown, which could affect Doubs' role, but he is open to flexible positioning.

title: DA: Mass. man walked into police station, told officers he killed his father
author: Carson Lyle
bullets: David Boucher, 48, charged with first-degree murder for allegedly killing his father in North Adams, Massachusetts.
Boucher reported the alleged crime at North Adams police station; the victim was found stabbed in a Walnut St. home.
Boucher pleaded not guilty, held without bail; pretrial hearing set for June 12 in Northern Berkshire District Court.

title: Worcester man, 62, killed by vehicle while crossing street, police say
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: James McGuire, 62, was fatally struck by a vehicle while crossing near 137 Hope Ave., Worcester.
The incident occurred shortly after 9 p.m. Sunday; McGuire died at a nearby hospital.
Worcester police's Crash Reconstruction Unit is investigating; no driver details or charges released.

title: Bruins’ Charlie McAvoy suspended 6 games for slashing Sabres’ Zach Benson
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Charlie McAvoy suspended six games for slashing Sabres' Zach Benson in season-ending game on May 1.
The incident led to McAvoy's third NHL suspension; previous infractions occurred in 2019 and 2023.
McAvoy, 28, led the Bruins in ice time and scored 61 points in 69 games last season.

title: TD Bank doubles its space amid return-to-office push
author: Beth Treffeisen
bullets: TD Bank expands to nearly 39,000 square feet at 2 International Place for a 10-year lease starting in 2027.
The new office can accommodate up to 170 employees, with plans for 140 to work there initially.
Mayor Wu supports office return, citing benefits for downtown businesses amid increased Boston office visits.

title: What would AJ Brown bring to the Patriots if they land him? All-Pro DB Kevin Byard knows.
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: Patriots are rumored to be interested in trading for Eagles receiver AJ Brown, a former Tennessee Titan.
Safety Kevin Byard praises AJ Brown as an 'incredible talent' and a true No. 1 receiver.
AJ Brown surpassed 1,000 yards in six of seven NFL seasons; Patriots lack a current No. 1 receiver.
